@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz CLS Diesel Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz CLS 220 / 250 CDI or 220d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLS diesel engine typically costs £2,000–£7,500 supply only depending on generation. OM651-era engines (C218) sit at the lower end (£2,000–£6,000), while OM654 units (C257) run £2,500–£7,500. A full engine replacement can run between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engines typically at the higher end of the range. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz CLS 220 / 250 CDI or 220d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your CLS has. The OM651 (C218, 2011–2018) is fundamentally reliable — 200,000–300,000 km examples are common — but has predictable weak points: timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical failure point. Early versions also suffered from Delphi piezo injector failures. The OM654 (C257, 2018–2021) is a more refined, lighter and generally more reliable engine, though timing chain wear and injector seal issues can still occur. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz CLS 220 / 250 CDI or 220d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch on the OM651 (C218) — the most critical failure; early Delphi piezo injector failure on OM651 units; EGR valve and cooler clogging on both OM651 and OM654; DPF clogging; and water pump leaks. The OM651 timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement labour-intensive.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz CLS diesel engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your CLS is worth £8,000–£15,000 and a recon engine is £4,000 (27–50%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £6,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. For OM654-engined cars (C257, 2018–2021), higher vehicle values make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz CLS diesel engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: C218 (2011–2018) with 220 CDI is OM651 (170 PS), 250 CDI is OM651 (204 PS); C257 (2018–2021) 220d is OM654 (194 PS). The CLS 220d replaced the earlier CLS 220 CDI model. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement (£2,000–£5,500+). The chain is located at the rear of the engine, making this a labour-intensive job. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil and filter changes with good quality oil of the recommended viscosity should ensure a long life.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz CLS 220 / 250 CDI & 220d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. A full engine replacement can run between £1,500 and £5,000 on average, with Mercedes engines typically at the higher end of the range. Reconditioned engines are available with 12-month warranties from UK suppliers. Older OM651 units generally sit at the lower end of the range; OM654 units command a premium.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLS diesel engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The OM651 timing chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement labour-intensive.

cta.label: → Compare CLS 220 / 250 CDI & 220d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (OM651 — C218 2011–2018)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or slapping noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, engine warning light, poor performance, rough idling. The OM651's single most critical failure point is timing chain stretch — if it stretches beyond tolerance or a guide fails, the consequences range from misfires and cam/crank correlation codes to catastrophic engine damage if the chain jumps a tooth. The timing chain drives both camshafts and the high-pressure fuel pump. Timing chains can be a weak point on the OM651, with tensioners often becoming noisy around 90,000 miles.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; if the chain jumps, it's an interference engine — catastrophic failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The OM651's timing chain is its single most critical failure point. Address rattling noises immediately to prevent severe engine damage. The timing chain sits at the rear of the engine, making replacement labour-intensive. Regular oil and filter changes with good quality oil of the recommended viscosity should ensure a long life. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately — preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine rebuild.

problems.title: Early Delphi Piezo Injector Failure (OM651 — 220 CDI & 250 CDI)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, decreased performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, fuel smell in the oil. The 220 CDI and 250 CDI variants were equipped with Piezo injectors, which became known for their high susceptibility to failure, resulting in symptoms such as strong vibrations during acceleration and increased consumption. Early versions of OM651 engines had faulty fuel injectors that were prone to premature wear-and-tear and a high failure rate.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (injector replacement). A new Delphi common rail pump can cost around £792.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, misfires can damage catalytic converters.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The early Delphi piezo injectors fitted to OM651 engines are sensitive to fuel quality and operating conditions. They can cause erratic fuel delivery, rough running, and higher consumption. If you experience rough idle or misfires, have the injectors inspected. Later OM651 engines received more durable magnetic solenoid injectors. Both problems were resolved by 2010.

problems.title: EGR Valve & Cooler Clogging (OM651 & OM654)
problems.symptoms: Reduced performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, limp mode, hesitation under load. Soot builds inside the intake and EGR passages, causing inconsistent airflow. A failed EGR valve can cause rough running and stalling.
problems.typicalMileage: 50,000–100,000 miles — accelerated by short urban journeys.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, performance will continue to degrade.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: Both the OM651 and OM654 are sensitive to EGR clogging, particularly with short urban journeys. The ECU compares requested vs measured air mass — when airflow becomes inconsistent, the car can enter limp mode. Regular motorway runs can help keep the EGR system clean. If you experience hesitation or a sudden drop in fuel economy, have the EGR system inspected promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: CLS 220 / 250 CDI & 220d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Clean or replace — do it when warnings appear
Replace affected injectors — upgrade to magnetic solenoid type
Forced regeneration or clean — do it when warning appears
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLS Diesel? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2018 OM654 (C257) with the updated engine design — more refined and efficient
* OM651 (C218) with full service history and regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* OM651 with the improved timing chain tensioner components
* Full service history with regular DPF-friendly driving (motorway miles)
avoidIf: * OM651 with cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is imminent
* OM651 with rough idle or misfires — early injector failure
* No service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* EGR or DPF warning lights — evidence of clogging issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLS diesel badge spans two generations of Mercedes engines. The OM651 (C218, 2011–2018) is fundamentally reliable but has predictable weak points — timing chain stretch from ~120,000 km is the single most critical issue. The OM654 (C257, 2018–2021) is a more refined, lighter and generally more reliable engine. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £4,000–£5,000 reconditioned engine on a CLS valued at £8,000–£15,000 is 27–50% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. On a sub-£6,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare CLS 220 / 250 CDI & 220d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz CLS 220 / 250 CDI or CLS 220d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (OM651 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM651/OM654) and oil-interval discipline
* Injector History — For OM651, check if injectors have been replaced
* DPF Status — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys accelerate clogging
* EGR System — Any hesitation or sudden drop in fuel economy? (EGR clogging)
* Smoke Check — Black smoke could indicate injector or DPF issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
